case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for atomic hydrogen on the provided surface.
case_metadata.notes: Strong slip in prior run; Mo3Pd benchmark

plan.reasoning: History shows only ontop Mo (best, -2.650 eV) and ontop Pd tested; both succeeded without slip or dissociation. Atomic H (SMILES [H]) is a radical, so ReactiveSpecies. Heavy-atom list is empty but binding atom is the sole H (index 0). To locate lower energy, test an unexamined high-symmetry site: hollow Mo-Mo-Mo. Predicted MACE-MP-0 energy for this new hollow configuration is slightly lower than the current ontop-Mo best.

History is empty so this is the first attempt. SMILES [H] is atomic hydrogen (radical). Use ReactiveSpecies with adsorbate_binding_indices=[0]. Start with the most common stable site for H on transition-metal surfaces: ontop Mo.
